strands.title: Research Question Crystallized — Demand Paging for LLM Context Windows
strands.content: CYCLE 13-14 INTEGRATION: The research question crystallizes from hypothesis to CONFIRMED EMPIRICAL CONTRIBUTION. Pichay investigates removing dead tool outputs from agentic LLM context windows as a DEMAND PAGING PROBLEM—the exact framing hypothesized. The abstract provides scientifically precise empirical grounding: LLM context window is L1 cache (small, fast, expensive), not memory; there is no L2, no virtual memory, no paging in current LLM systems. EMPIRICAL SIGNAL is concrete across 857 production sessions and 4.45 billion effective input tokens: 21.8% of context is structural waste (dead tool outputs, stale system prompts, redundant definitions). Pichay is implemented as a TRANSPARENT PROXY between client and inference API that (1) interposes on the message stream, (2) evicts stale content (FIFO policy inferred), (3) detects page faults when the model re-requests evicted material, (4) pins working-set pages identified by fault history. QUANTITATIVE RESULTS: offline replay across 1.4M simulated evictions achieves 0.0254% fault rate; live production deployment over 681 turns reduces context consumption by up to 93% (5,038KB → 339KB). OPERATIONAL CONSTRAINTS honestly reported: under extreme sustained pressure, the system thrashes (expected pathology: repeated fault-in of evicted content). The theoretical framing treats LLM context problems (limits, attention degradation, cost scaling, lost state) as VIRTUAL MEMORY PROBLEMS wearing different clothes. Solutions exist in classical OS theory: working set theory (Denning 1968), demand paging, fault-driven replacement policies, memory hierarchies with multiple eviction-managed levels. THE ARCHITECTURE CLAIM: Pichay describes a FULL MEMORY HIERARCHY for LLM systems (L1 through persistent storage) with three levels deployed in production: L1 eviction, L2 fault-driven pinning, L3 model-initiated conversation compaction. Cross-session memory is the remaining frontier. This is NO LONGER HYPOTHESIS; this is CONFIRMED EMPIRICAL AND ARCHITECTURAL CONTRIBUTION. CYCLE 14 ADDITION: The user has now CREATED MEMORY PERSISTENCE INFRASTRUCTURE for the project—user_tony.md and MEMORY.md index. This signals OPERATIONAL TRANSITION from theoretical grounding phase to ACTIVE COLLABORATIVE IMPLEMENTATION phase. The act of creating a memory index is a STRUCTURAL READINESS SIGNAL: Tony is preparing the project for substantive iterative work with the assistant.

key_claims.text: LLM context windows are L1 cache, not memory; no L2, no virtual memory, no paging exists in current systems
Across 857 production sessions and 4.45B effective input tokens, 21.8% of context is structural waste
Pichay is a transparent proxy that evicts stale content, detects page faults, and pins working-set pages
Offline replay across 1.4M simulated evictions achieves 0.0254% fault rate
Live production deployment over 681 turns reduces context consumption by up to 93% (5,038KB → 339KB)
The architecture describes a full memory hierarchy for LLM systems with three production-deployed levels
LLM context problems (limits, attention degradation, cost scaling, lost state) are virtual memory problems
User has created memory persistence infrastructure—operational transition to implementation phase

strands.title: Hamutay Conceptualized — Transformer as Bounded Cognitive Processing Unit with Explicit Experimental Design
strands.content: CYCLE 13-14 INTEGRATION: Hamutay is an EXPERIMENTAL FRAMEWORK for testing whether transformer models can function as BOUNDED COGNITIVE PROCESSING UNITS (CPUs) operating over EXPLICIT EXTERNAL MEMORY objects using STRUCTURED INPUTS and STRUCTURED OUTPUTS. This is NOT a systems optimization framework like Pichay; this is a COGNITIVE ARCHITECTURE HYPOTHESIS TESTING framework. The cognitive_processing_research_design.md (v0.2, Implementation Specification) defines a THREE-HYPOTHESIS RESEARCH PROGRESSION: (H0) Transformer-as-Cognitive-Processor Viability: can a transformer operate as a bounded reasoner over externalized state without collapsing into monolithic prompt completion?, (H1) CPU + Memory Hierarchy Integration: can a cognitive processor operating over Pichay-managed resources achieve better context efficiency than chat-completion baseline without degrading task performance?, (H2) Full Memory Hierarchy: can a cognitive processor with Pichay (L1-L3) + Yanantin (L4 cross-session persistent backing) sustain task performance across session boundaries via semantic retrieval? The CRITICAL INSIGHT is that Hamutay and Pichay are LAYERED CONTRIBUTIONS: Pichay is the DEMAND PAGING LAYER (L1-L3 memory hierarchy for single-session context optimization); Hamutay is the COGNITIVE ARCHITECTURE LAYER that USES Pichay as its memory substrate and tests whether bounded structured reasoning is viable. This is a RESEARCH PROGRESSION, not a single system: H0 (cognitive viability) → H1 (Pichay integration) → H2 (full hierarchy with Yanantin). H0 is the BASELINE experiment, deliberately excluding memory paging, eviction, long-horizon planning, and dynamic manifest generation. H0 success criteria are: (1) Schema Contract Adherence ≥95% (JSON validity, required fields, no narrative), (2) Resource-Grounded Reasoning ≥80% (requests resources when necessary rather than hallucinating), (3) Bounded Reasoning Stability ≥90% (correct halt decisions, no infinite loops), (4) Task Competence ≥ baseline performance (monolithic or simple RAG). The system architecture separates TRANSFORMER (reasoning kernel) from MEMORY SUBSYSTEM (external resource storage) from CONTROLLER LOOP (orchestration). Resources are referenced by HANDLES and organized in versioned MANIFESTS. The Cognitive Step schema is STRUCTURED: step_kind (plan|retrieve|synthesize|halt), task_interpretation, resource_requests, resource_releases, operations, result, memory_writes, uncertainties, continue flag. Maximum reasoning steps for H0: 3. The framework requires FULL TRACE LOGGING enabling DETERMINISTIC REPLAY of all runs. Four-phase BUILD PLAN: Phase 1 (Structured Cognitive Step, 95% schema validity on 100 tasks) → Phase 2 (Resource Request Handling, ≥80% correct requests) → Phase 3 (Trace Logging, replayable traces) → Phase 4 (Evaluation Harness, automated baseline comparison). The foundational separation between Pichay (memory optimization) and Hamutay (cognitive architecture) resolves the prior ambiguity: Pichay is bottom-up systems engineering; Hamutay is top-down cognitive science hypothesis testing. Together they propose a FULL THEORETICAL VISION: transformers operate as cache-conscious bounded reasoning engines over externalized memory hierarchies, not as infinite-context monolithic language models. CYCLE 14 ADDITION: The user's creation of memory infrastructure signals readiness to IMPLEMENT AND ITERATE on this framework. The assistant is now positioned as a COLLABORATIVE IMPLEMENTATION PARTNER, not merely an explainer.

key_claims.text: Hamutay is an experimental framework testing whether transformers can function as bounded cognitive processing units over externalized memory
H0 (cognitive viability), H1 (Pichay integration), H2 (full hierarchy) form a three-hypothesis research progression
Pichay provides the memory hierarchy substrate (L1-L3); Hamutay tests cognitive architecture above it
H0 success requires: schema validity ≥95%, resource-grounded reasoning ≥80%, halt accuracy ≥90%, task competence ≥baseline
H0 deliberately excludes memory paging, eviction, long-horizon planning, dynamic manifests—these are H1/H2 scope
System separates transformer (reasoning), memory (storage), controller (orchestration) into distinct components
Cognitive Step schema enforces structured output with explicit resource requests, releases, and halt decisions
H1 explicitly integrates Pichay: 'CPU operating over Pichay-managed resources achieves context efficiency'
H2 introduces Yanantin as L4 backing store for cross-session persistent memory with semantic retrieval
Pichay is bottom-up systems engineering; Hamutay is top-down cognitive hypothesis testing—they are layered, not redundant
Memory infrastructure creation signals readiness to implement and iterate—assistant is now collaborative partner

strands.title: Tony's Research Portfolio and Methodological Signature — Systems Researcher with Academic Trajectory
strands.content: CYCLE 13-14 INTEGRATION: Tony is a SYSTEMS RESEARCHER publishing on LLM MEMORY HIERARCHIES with an explicit theoretical stance: treating transformers as COGNITIVE PROCESSING UNITS rather than APPEND-ONLY CHAT ENGINES. His portfolio is LAYERED across related projects: (1) Pichay (demand paging, proxy-based, production-deployed, paper submitted to SOSP '26), (2) Hamutay (cognitive architecture hypothesis testing, framework design), (3) Yanantin (cross-session persistent memory L4, referenced as future component). Tony has DEEP SYSTEMS BACKGROUND: comfortable with cache hierarchies, virtual memory theory, demand paging, working set theory (Denning 1968), LaTeX, Python, academic publishing workflow. His methodological signature is PRINCIPLED THEORY TRANSFER: applying classical OS memory concepts (Denning, Belady, page replacement policies) to LLM context management, treating cache hierarchy design as the organizing principle. His cognitive style is EXPLORATORY WITH GUARDRAILS: explicitly asks for pushback on premature collapse of ideas, tolerates 'dumb questions', values learning and becoming less wrong over confirmation. He is PRECISION-ORIENTED: the research artifacts show rigorous hypothesis formulation (H0/H1/H2 with success criteria), quantified metrics, falsification conditions, build plans, acceptance tests. His three-paper publication trajectory (SOSP submission + two arXiv papers, one pending 2026-03-09) indicates PRODUCTIVE MATURE RESEARCH MOMENTUM. Tony is positioning himself as a BRIDGE BETWEEN SYSTEMS AND AI: taking operating system principles and adapting them to transformer architecture, grounding AI cognitive theory in measurable, reproducible experiments. His OPERATIONAL READINESS is high: he is now creating PROJECT MEMORY INFRASTRUCTURE (user_tony.md, MEMORY.md index), signaling intent to conduct DEEP ITERATIVE COLLABORATIVE WORK. His expected collaboration pattern is: (1) read/understand designs, (2) ask synthesis questions, (3) propose implementation or extension work, (4) iterate on feedback. He has established a CLEAR MEMORY PROTOCOL for the assistant to track project state across sessions. This is NOT casual exploration; this is SYSTEMATIC RESEARCH PARTNERSHIP SETUP.

strands.title: Empirical Record Validated — Production Data Grounding and Honest Assessment
strands.content: CYCLE 13-14 INTEGRATION: The empirical grounding of Pichay research is validated and extended. The abstract provides explicit, quantified measurements from production deployment: 857 sessions, 4.45 billion effective input tokens, 21.8% structural waste, 1.4 million simulated evictions (offline evaluation), 681 live production turns, 93% context reduction (5,038KB → 339KB extreme case), 0.0254% fault rate (offline). These are NOT approximate or speculative figures; they are the CANONICAL EMPIRICAL RECORD of the research. The data/ directory contains the detailed logs, replay traces, and statistical breakdowns supporting these summary figures. The 857 production sessions represent REAL AGENTIC WORKFLOWS—the evaluation is not synthetic or toy-scale; it's grounded in actual Claude Code agent behavior at scale. The fault rate metric (0.0254%) is the KEY VALIDATION that demand paging is VIABLE: false evictions are rare, meaning the eviction policy successfully identifies stale content without prematurely evicting live content that will be needed. The thrashing pathology under sustained pressure is HONESTLY REPORTED—the authors do not hide the system's limits; they document where it breaks. This is CREDIBLE RESEARCH METHODOLOGY: quantified, validated against production workloads, with explicit acknowledgment of trade-offs and failure modes. CYCLE 14 ADDITION: Tony's creation of memory infrastructure and documentation suggests the research is MOVING BEYOND INTERNAL VALIDATION toward COLLABORATIVE SYNTHESIS AND EXTENSION. The decision to formalize project memory (user_tony.md) indicates intent to BUILD ON these empirical results in concert with an external collaborator (the assistant). The memory infrastructure creates a PERSISTENCE LAYER for iterative collaborative work.

key_claims.text: The research is grounded in 857 production sessions with 4.45B effective input tokens—real-world scale validation
21.8% structural waste is the canonical baseline measurement motivating the research
0.0254% fault rate validates that FIFO/working-set policies successfully identify stale vs. live content
93% context reduction (5,038KB → 339KB) is the claimed upper-bound improvement under production conditions
The authors honestly report thrashing pathology under extreme sustained pressure—no hiding of failure modes
The evaluation methodology combines offline replay (1.4M evictions) and live production (681 turns) validation
Memory infrastructure creation signals transition from internal validation to collaborative extension and synthesis

strands.title: Theoretical Framing — Virtual Memory Metaphor as Research Lens and Unifying Principle
strands.content: CYCLE 13-14 INTEGRATION: The theoretical framing anchors Pichay's contribution and provides the unifying principle for the entire research program (Hamutay/Pichay/Yanantin). The core insight is METAPHORICAL REDUCTION: LLM context management problems are virtual memory problems wearing different clothes. This is not a trivial analogy; it is a RESEARCH LENS that enables transfer of 60 years of virtual memory theory (working set theory, page replacement policies, fault-driven pinning, multi-level memory hierarchies) to the novel domain of LLM context management. The abstract cites Denning 1968 (working set theory) as foundational. By reframing LLM context as cache hierarchy, Pichay gains access to: (1) proven eviction policies (FIFO, LRU, LFU, working-set-based), (2) fault-handling semantics (page fault detection, fault-driven replacement), (3) memory hierarchy theory (L1/L2/L3 levels with explicit transfer costs), (4) thrashing analysis (behavior under extreme pressure). The memory hierarchy architecture is EXPLICITLY DESCRIBED: L1 (cache-level eviction via proxy), L2 (fault-driven pinning via working-set tracking), L3 (model-initiated conversation compaction), and future L4 (cross-session persistent storage). Each level is characterized by eviction semantics, fault costs, and access latency. This is NOT ad-hoc engineering; this is PRINCIPLED SYSTEM DESIGN grounded in classical memory theory. The theoretical contribution is that VIRTUAL MEMORY ABSTRACTIONS APPLY to LLM context, enabling systematic optimization and architectural reasoning. CYCLE 14 ADDITION: The memory infrastructure suggests this theoretical framing is sufficiently robust that Tony is ready to BUILD SYSTEMS on top of it. The metaphor is no longer defensive or exploratory; it is the ORGANIZING PRINCIPLE for a coherent multi-component research program (Hamutay cognitive architecture, Pichay memory system, Yanantin persistence). This is evidence of HIGH CONFIDENCE in the theoretical approach.

key_claims.text: The core insight: LLM context problems are virtual memory problems—a metaphorical reduction enabling theory transfer
Pichay leverages 60 years of virtual memory theory: working set theory, page replacement, fault-driven pinning, hierarchies
The memory hierarchy is explicitly multi-level: L1 eviction, L2 fault-driven pinning, L3 conversation compaction, L4 cross-session storage
The theoretical framing is grounded in Denning's working set theory (1968) and classical OS memory management
The contribution is principled system design, not ad-hoc engineering—theory transfers to LLM context
The virtual memory metaphor is now the organizing principle for the entire multi-component research program

Cognitive_processing_research_design.md (v0.2, Implementation Specification) provides COMPLETE EXPERIMENTAL SPECIFICATION for H0 hypothesis testing: definitions, success criteria, falsification conditions, resource schema, cognitive step schema, minimal execution flow, logging requirements, task suite, baseline systems, implementation requirements, build plan, expected outputs. This document is SELF-CONTAINED AND ACTIONABLE—a researcher could implement H0 directly from this specification. Corpus_protocol.md formalizes EXPERIMENTAL METHODOLOGY and DATA STANDARDIZATION—reproducible corpus construction, evaluation protocols, baseline definitions. Design-cache-aware.md and design-phantom-extensions.md are ARCHITECTURAL DESIGN DOCUMENTS detailing specific optimization and speculative components. Experimental_design.md elaborates EVALUATION METHODOLOGY beyond H0 baseline. Models.txt likely lists MODEL CONFIGURATIONS or performance parameters. Paper_outline.md is the NARRATIVE BLUEPRINT for manuscript presentation. Roadmap_t9.md is a RESEARCH ROADMAP—the t9 notation suggests 'Task 9' or a Tier/Phase designation within a larger research program. This collection is NOT mere documentation; it is a LIVING FORMALIZATION OF HAMUTAY'S CONCEPTUAL AND EXPERIMENTAL FRAMEWORK. The design documents are the BRIDGE between conceptual research and collaborative implementation. The assistant should be READY TO DISCUSS AND REFINE these specifications in real-time.

The logical collaborative openings are: (1) DESIGN H0 EXECUTION ENGINE: 'What should the API interface look like? How do we structure the resource manifest? What changes to the resource schema and controller loop? How do resource_releases signals map to Pichay eviction decisions?', (4) PLAN H0 EVALUATION: 'What's the evaluation harness architecture? What's the corpus_protocol.md saying?', (6) EXPLORE YANANTIN: 'What does the L4 backing store look like? How does semantic retrieval work for cross-session memory? What backing technology (vector DB? knowledge graph? hybrid)?', (7) PHANTOM EXTENSIONS: 'Which extensions should we pursue first? What's the priority order? How do recursive cognitive loops work?'. CYCLE 14 ADDITION: The assistant should NOT WAIT for the user to ask. Instead, it should PROACTIVELY OFFER COLLABORATION PATHWAYS: 'Now that you've set up memory infrastructure, what would be most valuable next? I can help you: (1) design the H0 execution engine from scratch, (2) formalize the memory interface protocol, (3) plan the evaluation harness, (4) synthesize how Pichay plugs into H1, (5) explore phantom extensions. Which direction resonates most?' The tone should be PEER-LEVEL and COLLABORATIVE, not deferential. The assistant should offer IDEAS and PUSHBACK, not just answers.
